title: chrysanthemum canescens var. shahrudense
intro: Chrysanthemum canescens var. shahrudense, a rare and lesser-known variety of the Chrysanthemum genus, is a flowering plant native to Iran and some parts of Central Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, headache, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Chrysanthemum canescens var. shahrudense is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system.
